title: GASTAT Health Determinants Statistics Publication 2024 (Saudi Arabia)
abstract: National tables from GASTAT's Health Determinants Statistics Publication 2024, covering obesity and overweight (BMI) among adults and children, tobacco/e-cigarette consumption and passive smoke exposure, and nutrition statistics including fruit and vegetable consumption frequency, disaggregated by sex, nationality, age group, and administrative region.

resources.name: Health Determinants Statistics Publication 2024 (XLSX, 25 tables)
resources.path: KAUST - DATA/Human Nutrition and Health/Health Determinants Statistics Publication 2024 EN.xlsx

variables.name: obesity_prevalence_pct
variables.label: Obesity Prevalence
variables.description: Prevalence of obesity among adults (15+) or children (2-14), by sex, nationality, and age group/administrative region, from the National Health Survey 2024 / Woman and Child Health Survey 2024.

variables.name: fruit_veg_5plus_servings_pct
variables.label: Recommended Fruit & Vegetable Consumption
variables.description: Percentage of adults consuming the recommended 5+ servings of fruit and vegetables per day, by sex, nationality, and age group/administrative region.

access.how_to_request: Published openly by the General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT) on the Saudi Open Data platform / stats.gov.sa; no request required.

provenance.originator: General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT), Saudi Arabia
provenance.citation: GASTAT. 2024. Health Determinants Statistics Publication 2024. Riyadh: General Authority for Statistics.

known_issues: Multi-sheet XLSX with 25 tables in wide, human-readable (not tidy) layout; would require reshaping before analysis.

A near-duplicate file 'Health Determinants Statistics Publication 2024 EN (1).xlsx' (identical file size, 205,054 bytes) also exists in the same folder; only the canonical (non-'(1)') filename is cataloged as the resource — the two should be diffed/deduplicated before archiving.
No sub-national spatial detail beyond GASTAT's administrative regions, which do not align with Red Sea subbasin boundaries; applied uniformly across all five subbasins.
